parts.homeLangDetails: Identical in form and meaning to English "mis-," signalling that something is done incorrectly or badly. This is one of the easiest Danish prefixes for English speakers. Examples: misforstå (misunderstand), mislykkes (fail), mistro (distrust).
The same intensifying for- seen in forståelse — here it's simply part of the base word forståelse (understanding) that mis- is attached to.

Turns verbs and adjectives into abstract nouns, roughly like English "-ness" or "-ance." It is one of the most productive suffixes in formal and everyday Danish alike. Examples: følelse (feeling), forståelse (understanding), oplevelse (experience).

words.exampleSentence: Det var en total misforståelse fra min side.
words.exampleTranslation: It was a total misunderstanding on my part.
words.flag: 4 parts — borderline, but each is a meaningful morpheme in its own right

parts.homeLangDetails: Mirrors English "out-" almost perfectly, giving a sense of outward movement or completion. You will spot it constantly in spoken Danish because Danes love phrasal-verb-style compounds. Examples: udgang (exit), udvikle (develop), udtale (pronounce).
From the verb slette (to erase, to wipe out, to delete). Related to 'slet' meaning bad or plain — the core idea is reducing something to nothing.
The -t here is part of the participial stem of slette → udslettet, before the abstract noun suffix -else is added.

Turns verbs and adjectives into abstract nouns, roughly like English "-ness" or "-ance." It is one of the most productive suffixes in formal and everyday Danish alike. Examples: følelse (feeling), forståelse (understanding), oplevelse (experience).

words.exampleSentence: Krigen førte til udslettelse af hele landsbyer.
words.exampleTranslation: The war led to the annihilation of entire villages.

parts.homeLangDetails: An independent Danish word meaning 'self' or 'oneself', used as a compounding element much like English 'self-' in 'self-awareness'.
From 'bevidst' (conscious, aware). Related to 'vide' (to know) — so 'bevidst' is something like 'be-knowledged', i.e., aware or conscious.
The direct cognate of English "-hood" and German "-heit," it creates abstract nouns from adjectives describing a state or quality. Examples: frihed (freedom), skønhed (beauty), sandhed (truth).

words.exampleSentence: Selvbevidsthed er en vigtig egenskab for en leder.
words.exampleTranslation: Self-awareness is an important quality for a leader.
words.flag: 3 parts, but 'selvbevidsthed' could be further split (selv + be + vidst + hed); kept at 3 meaningful units for clarity
